div1.entryFree: Cleave, to stick, to hold to; followed by to: my tongue c. to my roof, R2 V, 3, 31. thy son's blood --ing to my blade, H6C I, 3, 50. Figuratively, a) to fit, to sit well on: new honours, like our strange garments, c. not to their mould, Mcb. I, 3, 145. b) to adhere closely, to stick to, to abide by: thy thoughts I c. to, Tp. IV, 165. and c. to no revenge but Lucius,' Tit. V, 2, 136. if you shall c. to my consent, it shall make honour for you, Mcb. II, 1, 25.
